mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.8% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor demand that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 4.8% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal savings rates indicate a supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, inflation readings above 3.5% force the market to price in prolonged restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations over a 1-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative mean difference when CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS are simultaneously elevated compared to baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarterly increase in real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive forward returns over a 1-week horizon as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in GDP growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 110.0, it signals a severe supply-side contraction that disrupts corporate input costs and logistics, driving negative equity returns over a 1-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the industrial production threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment and declining payroll employment indicates a synchronized labor market breakdown that accelerates recession probability, leading to negative equity returns over a 1-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of housing market stress, a decline in housing starts below 1.4 million units reduces consumer wealth and construction-related corporate revenues, depressing equity valuations over a 1-week horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in housing downturns are not significantly lower than baseline.
